key_points: Pressure depends on the force applied and the area over which it acts
Atmospheric pressure is caused by the weight of the air above us
Air moves from regions of high pressure to low pressure creating winds
Heating of air causes it to rise and form low-pressure areas
Differences in temperature and pressure in the atmosphere lead to wind formation
Cyclones develop when warm moist air rises over warm ocean waters creating a low-pressure center
examples: Sharp nails penetrate wood easily due to high pressure on a small area
Wide tyres of tractors reduce pressure on the ground
Warm air rising over oceans can lead to cyclone formation
Barometers are used to measure atmospheric pressure
formulas: Wind movement: Air flows from high pressure region to low pressure region
exam_tips: Explain clearly how pressure changes with force and area
Understand the relationship between temperature, pressure and wind formation
Learn the conditions required for cyclone formation
Use diagrams when explaining cyclone structure and wind movement
